Data Source: Feeding America – Map the Meal Gap
Description: Provides county level food insecurity rates, number of food-insecure individuals, and meal gap estimates across the U.S. students can use this data to identify high need areas and connect chatbot users to resource
How NourishNet uses this data source: NourishNet uses this to identify the high priority regions with food insecurty.

Description: Statewide datasets including demographics, schools, community resources, and environmental data that can support chatbot responses related to food access
How NourishNet uses this data source: These datasets used for regional analysis and to identify community facilities and infrastructure used in GIS-based food access mapping.

Description: Contains datasets on community resources, food assistance programs, demographics, and public services in Washington DC. Useful for DMV regional coverage
How NourishNet uses this data source: Used this datsset to find regional infrastructure for food divergance pathways such as to find the food distribution sites, etc.

Description: Includes datasets related to community services, public programs, and regional infrastructure in Virginia. Students can use this to build DMV-wide food access solutions
How NourishNet uses this data source: uses this portal as a resource for accessing datasets related to food access

Data Source: Prince George’s County open data portal
Description: Includes local datasets such as schools, public facilities, community resources, and neighborhood statistics useful for NourishNet regional analysis
How NourishNet uses this data source: Used for the development of GIS map for PG county

Data Source: Capital Area Food Bank Heat Map
Description: Interactive map showing food insecurity levels, distribution partners, and priority areas served by the CAFB across the DMV region
How NourishNet uses this data source: Used the CAFB interactive map to locate food distribution centers in DMV aera

Description: Provides county-level and regional data on hunger, food insecurity, and food distribution in Maryland. Useful for identifying communities with higher need
How NourishNet uses this data source: This dataset used to locate food assistance providers and food pantries accross DMV aera.

Description: Interactive map developed by the Maryland Food Bank showing food insecurity levels across Maryland counties. It includes statistics on households experiencing food insecurity, poverty indicators, and regional hunger trends.
How NourishNet uses this data source: to locate the food pantries in DMV aera

Description: Provides data on food availability, health indicators, food assistance participation, and local food systems. Useful for analyzing broader food system conditions.
How NourishNet uses this data source: Used for GIS analysis and evaluate food access conditions in regions such as Prince George’s County.

Data Source: US Census Bureau – American Community Survey
Description: Provides demographic, income, poverty, and housing data that can help identify communities vulnerable to food insecurity.
How NourishNet uses this data source: This dataset used as a resource for demographic and socioeconomic analysis related to food insecurity and community vulnerability.

Description: Resource directory maintained by the University of Maryland that lists food access programs, food pantries, SNAP resources, and community support services across Maryland
How NourishNet uses this data source: NourishNet uses this resource directory to identify food access programs and gather supporting data sources related to food assistance services

Description: Interactive map showinmg the food recovery organizations composting ficilities and anarobic digestion infrastructure accross the US.
How NourishNet uses this data source: NourishNet uses this dataset to identify food recovery infrastructure and estimate environmental impacts associated with different food waste diversion pathways

Data Source: PG County Healthy food priority areas
List of all the food pantries
How NourishNet uses this data source: We use this data to find the nearest food pantries based on location.
We need to decide if the food needs to be donated or it has to be diverted based on the distance, cost and CO2 emisions and etc.
